name: Brad Mehldau
description: American jazz pianist, composer, and arranger
abstract: Bradford Alexander Mehldau (; born August 23, 1970) is an American jazz pianist, composer, and arranger. Mehldau studied music at The New School, touring and recording while still a student.

about_quotes.speaker: Christine Jensen
about_quotes.evidence: I’m like that’s great but man there’s so much history in Roy’s playing coming from the masters. So I think that’s the great thing about education is that we do get to share some reality checks on the history and the pedagogy of the music in a learning space.
about_quotes.doc_title: Christine Jensen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 4, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Rick Margitza
about_quotes.evidence: they’re into Brad Mehldau. Right? Which I guess that’s kind of a natural generational thing, although we’re also telling these young guys before you go to Brad you have to check out Wynton, because Brad checked out Wynton.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rick Margitza interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 22, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Gordon Lorraine
about_quotes.evidence: But I sit and I listen to them, and I remember them, and sometimes they become leaders. Lots of times, from out of a group comes a leader. Brad Mehldau.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gordon Lorraine — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Joey said, 'Who do you listen to?' He said, 'Bill Evans, Keith Jarrett, Brad Mehldau and you.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
